claim: Vercel Agent Browser CLI achieves 95% first-try task completion vs 75-80% for Playwright MCP and Chrome DevTools MCP through smarter deterministic references
actionable: Use Vercel Agent Browser over Playwright MCP for browser automation in agents; the difference is measurable (95% vs 75-80% success rates)
source_title: Your AI Coding Workflow NEEDS This New Agent Browser CLI

claim: Agent Browser uses snapshot + condensed references (@e1, @e2) instead of selectors/searching; this 'less is more' approach is more deterministic and token-efficient
actionable: When designing browser automation tools for agents, return condensed deterministic references rather than forcing agents to search/match selectors
source_title: Your AI Coding Workflow NEEDS This New Agent Browser CLI

claim: Less is more: reducing tool options (17 specialized tools to 2 general tools) improved text-to-SQL agent success from 80% to 100%; applies to all agent tools
actionable: Minimize tool set and make tools flexible; don't create specialized tools for each use case; let agents compose general tools creatively
source_title: Your AI Coding Workflow NEEDS This New Agent Browser CLI

claim: Browser validation workflow should include regression testing on all user journeys after feature implementations to catch side effects
actionable: After agent implements a feature, run full user journey regression tests in browser before shipping; automation should validate this
source_title: Your AI Coding Workflow NEEDS This New Agent Browser CLI

claim: On complex websites (Amazon, multi-step flows), Agent Browser outperforms Playwright MCP significantly; simple pages see less difference in reliability
actionable: Use Agent Browser for complex site automation; Playwright is acceptable for simple pages but consider Agent Browser for production
source_title: Your AI Coding Workflow NEEDS This New Agent Browser CLI
